title: Crisp Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Crisp** is a customer messaging + helpdesk platform exposing a REST API at `https://api.crisp.chat/v1`. This connector — `CrispConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "crisp"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Crisp website:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| User / Account | `/user/account`, `/user/websites` | Authenticated profile + accessible websites |
| Website | `/website/{wid}` | Read website settings |
| Conversations | `/website/{wid}/conversations/{page}` + `/website/{wid}/conversation/{sid}` | List, get, send message |
| People | `/website/{wid}/people/profiles/{page}` + `/website/{wid}/people/profile/{pid}` | List, get, create, update contacts |
| Helpdesk | `/website/{wid}/helpdesk/locale/{locale}/articles/{page}` | List helpdesk articles |
| Campaigns | `/website/{wid}/campaigns/list/{page}` | List campaigns |

The connector normalises conversations + people + helpdesk articles into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429 / 5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::CrispHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Create a Crisp plugin

1. Sign in to https://app.crisp.chat.
2. Open **Marketplace → Develop → Manage plugins** (or visit https://marketplace.crisp.chat).
3. Click **New plugin**, fill in name + description, and grant the scopes you need (people:read, people:write, conversation:read, conversation:write, helpdesk:read, etc.).
4. From the plugin page → **Tokens** tab, copy the **Identifier** and **Key**. Crisp shows the Key only once.
5. From the main Crisp app → **Settings → Website Settings**, copy the **Website ID** (UUID).

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Crisp**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `identifier` (text) | Plugin identifier from step 4 |
| `api_key` (secret) | Plugin key from step 4 |
| `website_id` (text) | Website UUID from step 5 |
| `tier` (text, optional) | Defaults to `plugin` — only change to `user` for personal-token integrations |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.crisp.chat/v1` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `identifier` + `api_key` + `website_id` are present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `CrispHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which fetches the authenticated account via `GET /user/account` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Crisp REST API uses **HTTP Basic plugin authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `identifier` is sent as the Basic-auth **username** half.
- `api_key` is sent as the Basic-auth **password** half.
- `tier` is sent in the `X-Crisp-Tier` header — almost always `plugin`; `user` for personal user tokens.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `identifier` + `api_key` + `website_id`. Initialises `CrispHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Crisp endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a synthesised `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the precomputed `base64(identifier:api_key)` string and `token_type` is `"Basic"`. No real exchange happens. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /user/account` — minimal authenticated probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None — credentials live in `self.config` only. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Basic base64(identifier:api_key)
X-Crisp-Tier:  plugin                          (or "user")
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `identifier` | text | yes | `self.identifier` | Basic-auth username half |
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | Basic-auth password half |
| `website_id` | text | yes | `self.website_id` | Default Crisp website UUID for site-scoped methods |
| `tier` | text | no | `self.tier` (defaults to `"plugin"`) | `X-Crisp-Tier` header value |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `"https://api.crisp.chat/v1"`) | Override for Crisp sandbox / proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default `60`) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["identifier", "api_key", "website_id"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_CRISP_BASE = "https://api.crisp.chat/v1"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `CrispConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::CrispHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `identifier` + `api_key` + `website_id`; initialise `CrispHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for Basic auth — returns a synthesised `TokenInfo(token_type="Basic")` whose `access_token` is the precomputed base64-encoded credential.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Fetches `GET /user/account` as a lightweight probe. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates helpdesk articles → conversations → people for the configured `website_id`, normalises each into `NormalizedDocument`, and ingests via `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: User / Account
children.content:
### `async get_account_profile() -> Dict`
`GET /user/account`.

### `async list_websites() -> Dict`
`GET /user/websites`.

### `async get_website(website_id) -> Dict`
`GET /website/{website_id}`.

children.title: Conversations
children.content:
### `async list_conversations(website_id, page=1, per_page=50, search_query=None, search_filter_type=None) -> Dict`
`GET /website/{wid}/conversations/{page}?per_page={N}&search_query={Q}&search_type={T}`.

### `async get_conversation(website_id, session_id) -> Dict`
`GET /website/{wid}/conversation/{sid}`.

### `async send_message(website_id, session_id, type, from_, origin, content) -> Dict`
`POST /website/{wid}/conversation/{sid}/message` — body `{type, from, origin, content}`. The Python parameter `from_` is renamed to `from` on the wire to avoid the Python reserved word.

children.title: People (Contacts)
children.content:
### `async list_people(website_id, page=1, per_page=50, search_text=None, search_filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /website/{wid}/people/profiles/{page}?per_page={N}&search_text={S}`.

### `async get_person(website_id, people_id) -> Dict`
`GET /website/{wid}/people/profile/{pid}`.

### `async create_person(website_id, email=None, person=None, segments=None) -> Dict`
`POST /website/{wid}/people/profile` — body contains only the non-None fields among `email` / `person` / `segments`.

### `async update_person(website_id, people_id, person) -> Dict`
`PATCH /website/{wid}/people/profile/{pid}` — body `{person: {...}}`.

children.title: Helpdesk · Campaigns
children.content:
### `async list_helpdesks(website_id, locale="en", page=1) -> Dict`
`GET /website/{wid}/helpdesk/locale/{locale}/articles/{page}`.

### `async list_campaigns(website_id, page=1) -> Dict`
`GET /website/{wid}/campaigns/list/{page}`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
CrispError                            # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── CrispAuthError                    # 401 / 403
├── CrispBadRequestError              # 400
├── CrispNotFoundError                # 404
├── CrispConflictError                # 409 (duplicate contact email)
├── CrispRateLimitError               # 429 — retry_after_s
└── CrispServerError                  # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
CrispNetworkError = CrispServerError  # legacy name from older code
CrispNotFound     = CrispNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s), retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `CrispNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `identifier`, `api_key` or `website_id` blank | Re-enter all three in ACP. Crisp shows the Key only once at generation — rotate the plugin if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — Basic credentials rejected | Verify `identifier` + `api_key` came from the same plugin. Rotate the key in Marketplace and re-paste. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — plugin lacks required scope | Open the plugin in Marketplace → Permissions, add the scopes you need (e.g. `website:conversation:sessions:read`, `website:people:write`). |
| `crisp.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Crisp plugin rate cap) | Client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on a site-scoped call | Wrong `website_id` or resource doesn't exist on this site | Verify the Website ID matches the workspace the plugin token was issued for. |
| `send_message` body rejected with `reason: invalid_type` | Crisp's allowed values for `type` are `text`/`file`/`animation`/`audio`/`picker`/`field`/`carousel` | Pass one of the allowed kinds; `content` shape changes per `type`. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'CrispNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `CrispNetworkError = CrispServerError` and `CrispNotFound = CrispNotFoundError` aliases are in `exceptions.py`. |
